controls.requirement: Create and retain system audit logs/records to enable monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized activity.
controls.evidence: 5 device-reported receipt(s) retained across 3 device(s) as hash-chained, signed envelope rollups (5 verified / 0 failed at the device, per-envelope self-reported counts); 0 of 5 envelope(s) fleet-wide failed THIS module's own local re-verification (tampering/corruption); 1 device(s) with a broken chain, 1 with a seq-continuity gap, 1 silent (unreachable). Fleet-wide coverage-completeness is itself attested per device from re-verified envelope bytes, not asserted.

controls.requirement: Ensure the actions of individual system users can be uniquely traced to those users so they can be held accountable.
controls.evidence: 3 distinct pseudonymous operator ref(s) across 3 device(s) fleet-wide (HMAC-pseudonymized at the aggregator — individual identities never leave the originating device). Accountability is traceable to a stable per-window pseudonym, not a directly identifiable individual; this is the privacy design working, not a shortfall to fix.
3 device(s) contributing envelope rollups fleet-wide; the periodic review and update of which events to log remains an organizational process outside kriya.

controls.control: 3.3.4 (AU.L2-3.3.4 · AU-5) — Audit logging process failure alerting
controls.requirement: Alert in the event of an audit logging process failure.
controls.evidence: 1 device(s) currently silent out of 3, named: server-c (ed4928c628d1…). A stopped or silenced device is visible by absence in the signed coverage chain — a gap in the heartbeat/envelope stream, not a quiet nothing. No external paging/alerting integration exists.

controls.requirement: Correlate audit record review, analysis, and reporting processes for investigation and response to indications of suspicious activity.
controls.evidence: Cross-device correlation via kriyad's aggregated envelope store across 3 device(s); this is fleet-wide, single-organization correlation, not cross-organization SIEM aggregation.

controls.control: 3.3.6 (AU.L2-3.3.6 · AU-7) — Audit record reduction & report generation
controls.requirement: Provide audit record reduction and report generation to support on-demand analysis and reporting.
controls.evidence: This Markdown + JSON org-wide evidence bundle is itself the fleet-wide reduction/report artifact, generated on-demand from every device's signed envelope stream and independently re-verifiable offline.

controls.control: 3.3.7 (AU.L2-3.3.7 · AU-8) — Clock synchronization for time stamps
controls.requirement: Provide a system capability that compares and synchronizes internal system clocks with an authoritative source for audit-record time stamps.
controls.evidence: Every envelope carries a compiler-stamped window (from_ms/to_ms); clock synchronization against an authoritative source is OS-provided (NTP) on each device, outside kriya's control — this control is capped at partial regardless of fleet size.

controls.requirement: Protect audit information and audit logging tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion.
controls.evidence: 0 envelope(s) failed local re-verification fleet-wide out of 5 received (tampering/corruption detected) — the detection control is functioning as intended; investigate any flagged device(s). Access control over kriyad's own store/OS account is outside kriya's control, capping this at partial regardless of trail size.

controls.control: 3.3.9 (AU.L2-3.3.9 · AU-9(4)) — Limit audit-logging management to privileged users
controls.requirement: Limit management of audit logging functionality to a subset of privileged users.
controls.evidence: kriyad's aggregator runs under the operator's own OS account, and in-app roles are self-asserted (see docs/TRUST.md) — kriya enforces no privileged-user restriction on who can manage audit logging fleet-wide; this must be enforced by an OS-level or organizational access control (the same permanent gap the per-device engine reports, doc 21 Part D).

controls.requirement: Establish and maintain baseline configurations and inventories of systems throughout the respective system development life cycles.
controls.evidence: Policy bundle v2, authored and org-key-signed, is the fleet's current baseline; verified-applied on 1/3 device(s) (1 never applied any bundle within the window) — applied version taken from EACH DEVICE'S OWN locally re-verified envelope, never kriyad's serving hint.

controls.requirement: Establish and enforce security configuration settings for information technology products employed in organizational systems.
controls.evidence: 2 device(s) behind the current baseline: laptop-b (8139770ea87d…): applied v1 < latest v2; server-c (ed4928c628d1…): never applied (baseline is v2). Enriched with §7 inventory where available (console/runtime versions on `device_completeness`).

controls.requirement: Enforce approved authorizations for controlling the flow of information.
controls.evidence: A signed, per-decision enforcement point on governed connector lanes, fleet-wide: 0 kriya.io.* receipt(s) verified across 3 device(s) (0 allow, 0 deny, 0 approve). Nothing at this layer stands in the way of a flow that avoids it entirely — a spawned subprocess bypasses it on every device (see TRUST.md).
